title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox defeat Yankees 4-1, marking their third consecutive win in the series against A.L.-leading Yankees.
Masataka Yoshida and Anthony Seigler both hit home runs, aiding Boston's early lead against Gerrit Cole.
Jake Bennett pitched 6.1 innings, only allowing three hits as he secured his second career win for the Red Sox.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins trade 2021 first-round pick Fabian Lysell to the Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Ivan Ivan scored 26 points in 66 AHL games last season and has nine NHL points in 49 games.
Fabian Lysell, drafted 21st overall in 2021, played only 12 NHL games, scoring one goal and two assists.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup match at Gillette Stadium, excelling with an entertaining offensive performance.
Ousmane Dembélé scored a historic hat-trick, while Kylian Mbappe provided two assists, highlighting France's attacking prowess.
France, based in Boston for the 2026 World Cup, remains the tournament favorite due to their dynamic and high-caliber offense.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich Middle High School is under investigation after a student allegedly made specific threats against two classmates.
The threat was detected by the GoGuardian app on a school-issued device, prompting immediate police and school action.
The involved student is banned from school for the year while the Sandwich Police and DA's Office continue their investigation.

title: Man convicted after breaking into the wrong Boston apartment and murdering resident
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Victor Arrington, 40, was found guilty of first-degree murder and related charges in a 2015 Dorchester home invasion.
Prosecutors said Arrington and accomplices mistakenly targeted the wrong apartment, killing Richard Long and injuring his fiancée.
Arrington's retrial conviction included digital evidence from his iPhone's Frequent Location History feature used by prosecutors.

title: Some 20 New Englanders are on ‘American Ninja Warrior’ this season. Here’s your in-depth preview.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Massachusetts has become a hub for 'American Ninja Warrior' talent, with 18 New Englanders competing in Season 18.
Taylor Johnson from Bridgewater recently won the adult female division at the World Ninja League Championships.
New England's success is attributed to its numerous ninja gyms and influential coaches like Jordan Thurston.

title: Scotland came to Boston. Did romance follow?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Scottish tourists flooded Boston for the World Cup, creating a lively, social atmosphere that Bostonians enjoyed.
At a Scottish-themed restaurant, Scottish fans were observed enjoying their time despite a soccer game loss to Morocco.
Meredith Goldstein reports on potential romances between locals and Scottish visitors, inviting stories of connections made.

title: 4 of the weirdest, most absurd soccer movies streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Shaolin Soccer' combines soccer with Shaolin kung fu, blending sports and comedy on Paramount+, Tubi, Pluto TV, and The Roku Channel.
'Diamantino' offers a political satire of a Portuguese soccer superstar, available on Kanopy and Hoopla.
'United Passions', with a significant budget and minimal earnings, is a historically infamous film on Tubi, The Roku Channel, Plex, and The CW.

title: Is the Trump administration on the verge of further endangering North Atlantic right whales?
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Trump administration considers removing maritime slow zones designed to protect endangered North Atlantic right whales.
Proposed deregulation could replace static slow zones with dynamic, technology-driven alternatives, raising concerns among conservationists.
NOAA received over 29,000 public comments on potential changes; specifics of new rules unclear after comment period closed.

title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched seven scoreless innings for the Red Sox, allowing one hit and striking out seven against the Yankees.
Tolle carried a perfect game into the sixth inning before allowing his first and only hit at Fenway Park.
Despite battling an illness, Tolle has maintained a 2.78 ERA over 12 starts this season, solidifying his role in the rotation.
